Job Title: Data Engineer (Azure & Microsoft Fabric)

Work Location: Infopark, Kochi Experience Required: 4–5 Years Employment Type: Full-Time, Permanent Work Mode: Onsite, Hybrid Shift: Regular Shift

Company Description

SparkSupport Infotech Pvt Ltd, a leading IT solutions provider based in India, has joined forces with Tible, Netherlands, in a strategic joint venture to enhance our service offerings and expand our global footprint. For over 24 years, SparkSupport has been a trusted offshore technology partner for businesses in the UAE, UK, US, Netherlands, Canada, and other regions. We specialize in delivering scalable web and mobile applications, end-to-end cloud computing solutions, DevOps, data analytics, and Power Platform services. At SparkSupport, you will be part of a passionate team committed to solving tough technology challenges with innovative solutions, strengthened by our global partnership.

Role Description

We are seeking a highly skilled Data Engineer (Azure & Microsoft Fabric) with 4–5 years of experience in building scalable and efficient data engineering solutions using Microsoft Azure and Microsoft Fabric technologies. The ideal candidate should have strong expertise in ETL/ELT development, Azure Data Factory, Microsoft Fabric, advanced SQL, Data Lake architecture, and enterprise data pipeline orchestration. The role involves designing and implementing modern cloud-based data platforms, building robust ingestion frameworks including watermark/incremental loading, and enabling analytics and reporting solutions across the organization.

Key Responsibilities

  • Design, develop, and maintain scalable ETL/ELT pipelines using Microsoft Azure services and Microsoft Fabric.
  • Implement watermark-based, incremental loading, and CDC (Change Data Capture) mechanisms for optimized data ingestion and processing.
  • Develop and orchestrate end-to-end data workflows using Microsoft Azure Data Factory (ADF) and Fabric Pipelines.
  • Build and manage enterprise-scale data pipelines for structured, semi-structured, and unstructured data.
  • Design and implement Lakehouse and Data Warehouse solutions using Microsoft Fabric.
  • Work with OneLake architecture for centralized and governed enterprise data management.
  • Implement medallion architecture (Bronze, Silver, Gold layers) for scalable data transformation and analytics.
  • Develop and optimize Spark and SQL-based data transformations within Fabric environments.
  • Design, manage, and optimize Azure Data Lake solutions.
  • Write and optimize complex SQL queries, stored procedures, views, and performance tuning scripts.
  • Integrate data from multiple cloud and on-premise data sources.
  • Monitor, troubleshoot, and optimize pipeline execution, workload performance, and data quality.
  • Implement security, governance, lineage, and access controls across data platforms.
  • Collaborate with business analysts, BI developers, architects, and stakeholders to deliver data-driven solutions.
  • Support CI/CD implementation, Git integration, and deployment automation for data engineering projects.

Required Skills & Qualifications

Mandatory Skills: - Microsoft Azure & ETL: 4–5 years of experience in Data Engineering. - Strong hands-on experience in: - ETL/ELT development using Azure technologies - Microsoft Azure Data Factory (ADF) - Azure Data Lake - Data pipeline orchestration - Advanced SQL programming - Experience implementing: - Watermark-based loading - Incremental data processing - CDC frameworks - Batch and near real-time data ingestion - Strong understanding of: - Data warehousing concepts - Data modeling - Data transformation frameworks - Performance optimization techniques

Microsoft Fabric Skills: - Strong hands-on experience with: - Fabric Data Factory - Fabric Lakehouse - Fabric Warehouse - OneLake - Fabric Pipelines - Dataflows Gen2 - Fabric Notebooks - Experience designing modern analytics and enterprise data solutions using Microsoft Fabric. - Strong understanding of: - Medallion Architecture - Delta Tables - Parquet file formats - Data partitioning - Incremental processing - OneLake shortcuts - Experience integrating: - Power BI with Fabric Semantic Models - Azure services with Fabric ecosystem - SQL databases, APIs, and external data sources - Experience optimizing Spark jobs and SQL transformations in Fabric environments.

Preferred Skills: - Experience with: - PySpark - Python - Azure Synapse Analytics - Power BI - Git and CI/CD pipelines - Knowledge of: - Fabric administration and tenant settings - Workspace security and governance - Row-Level Security (RLS) - Data lineage and monitoring - Agile/Scrum methodologies

Educational Qualification

  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, Engineering, or related field.

Soft Skills

  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
  • Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities.
  • Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.
  • Strong attention to detail and commitment to data quality and governance.

Nice to Have

  • Microsoft Azure or Microsoft Fabric certifications.
  • Experience working on enterprise-scale cloud data platforms.
  • Exposure to Real-Time Analytics/Eventstream capabilities in Microsoft Fabric.

Contact: If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@sparksupport.com.
summary: Designs and implements cloud-based data solutions using Azure and Microsoft Fabric. Collaborates with teams to build scalable pipelines and analytics frameworks.

Role Overview

We seek a motivated and proactive Junior DevOps Engineer to support and enhance our cloud-native infrastructure and DevOps practices. The role involves working with modern cloud platforms, containerized environments, CI/CD pipelines, and monitoring systems to ensure high availability, scalability, and reliability of applications. The ideal candidate should be hands-on, eager to learn, collaborative, and passionate about automation, cloud technologies, and system reliability.

Key Responsibilities

  • Assist in building, deploying, and maintaining cloud infrastructure and containerized applications.
  • Work with AWS services (Fargate, ECS, DynamoDB, SNS, etc.) for managing distributed systems.
  • Monitor system performance using CloudWatch, Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, and Splunk, and respond to alerts.
  • Support CI/CD pipelines using tools like Jenkins, GitHub Actions, and ArgoCD.
  • Troubleshoot issues across infrastructure, networking, containers, and applications.
  • Assist in configuring and managing Kubernetes clusters, including deployments, services, and ingress.
  • Work on networking components such as load balancers, API gateways, reverse proxies, and service communication.
  • Participate in incident response, root cause analysis (RCA), and postmortems.
  • Automate repetitive tasks using scripts and tools to improve efficiency.
  • Maintain documentation for infrastructure, deployments, and troubleshooting processes.
  • Collaborate with development and operations teams to improve system reliability and deployment workflows.
  • Participate in on-call or shift rotations to ensure system availability.

Preferred Skills

Cloud Platforms and Services

  • Basic to intermediate knowledge of AWS services (ECS, Fargate, DynamoDB, SNS).
  • Basic to intermediate knowledge of GCP services (GKE, GAR, CloudSQL, Cloud Run).
  • Understanding of cloud-native architectures and distributed systems.
  • Familiarity with infrastructure provisioning concepts (Terraform/CloudFormation is a plus).

Monitoring and Logging Tools

  • Knowledge of CloudWatch Logs, Loki, Splunk for log analysis.
  • Familiarity with Prometheus and Grafana for metrics monitoring and dashboarding.
  • Understanding of alerting systems (Alertmanager) and incident response workflows.
  • Ability to analyze logs and metrics to identify system issues.

job_title: Security Engineer (Required Skills - Application Security & OWASP)
job_description:
H&R Block India (P) Ltd

Location: 211 N – 211 S, 11th Floor, Yamuna Building, Technopark Phase III, Kulathoor PO, Thiruvananthapuram, 695583 Website: www.hrblock.com Contact Email: anu.vishnu@hrblock.com Closing Date: 30 June 2026 Job Published: 22 May 2026

Responsibilities:

SDLC Integration and AppSec Tooling

  • Support day-to-day operation of application security tools (SAST, DAST, SCA, secrets detection, container image scanning).
  • Onboard new applications/repositories into AppSec tooling; configure scan policies and validate pipeline instrumentation.
  • Tune detection rules/policies to reduce false positives and improve signal quality for engineering teams.
  • Maintain documentation, runbooks, and quick-reference guides for AppSec tooling and processes.

Findings Triage and Vulnerability Management

  • Triage AppSec findings: validate, prioritize by risk/exploitability, deduplicate, and route to engineering owners.
  • Validate false positives via code context, data flow, and exploitability analysis; document rationale.
  • Improve scan quality by feeding false positive patterns and suppression decisions into tooling configuration and playbooks.
  • Track open vulnerabilities to closure; follow up on aging findings and SLA adherence.
  • Generate metrics on AppSec coverage, false positive rates, remediation trends, and emerging risks.
  • Coordinate remediation for high-impact open-source/supply-chain vulnerabilities (e.g., critical CVEs).

Secure Code Review and Developer Enablement

  • Conduct secure code reviews under senior architects' guidance, focusing on OWASP Top 10 and common weaknesses.
  • Partner with developers to explain findings, recommend fixes, and answer security questions.
  • Contribute to secure coding guidelines, checklists, and framework-specific guidance.
  • Support delivery of developer security awareness/training (e.g., lunch-and-learns, labs, onboarding modules).

Collaboration and Communication

  • Collaborate with engineering, platform, DevOps, and operations teams globally.
  • Communicate findings, risks, and recommendations to developers and managers.
  • Document triage decisions, exceptions, and remediation tracking for audit purposes.
  • Develop technical skills via mentorship, hands-on practice, and self-directed learning.

Qualifications:

Required

  • 2-3.5 years of experience in application security, software development, or related technical roles.
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Security, Software Engineering, or related fields.
  • Working knowledge of OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities and their manifestation in web/API applications.
  • Proficiency in at least one programming language (Python, Java, C#, JavaScript/TypeScript, Go) to analyze code and discuss findings.
  • Familiarity with Git, code review workflows, CI/CD pipelines, authentication/authorization/encryption concepts.
  • Strong analytical/problem-solving skills and attention to detail.
  • Effective communication skills for technical/non-technical audiences.
  • Demonstrated self-driven learning and rapid growth in AppSec depth over 12–18 months.

Preferred

  • Hands-on experience with AppSec tools (SAST, DAST, SCA, etc.).
  • Familiarity with threat modeling (STRIDE) and frameworks (OWASP ASVS, SAMM, NIST SSDF).
  • Exposure to cloud-native security (containers, Kubernetes, serverless, IaC).
  • Awareness of AI/ML security risks and AI-assisted development practices.
  • Participation in CTFs, bug bounties, security research, or open-source contributions.
  • Industry certifications (Security+, GIAC GFACT/GSEC) or progress toward them.
  • Experience in regulated industries (financial services, insurance, healthcare).
summary: Security Engineer role at H&R Block India focuses on application security tooling, vulnerability management, and developer enablement. Requires 2-3.5 years of experience with OWASP expertise and AppSec tool proficiency.
